name: Attenuverter
description: This simple algorithm scales and offsets signals. It is probably most useful for CVs, but can be used for audio as well. The signal is scaled and then offset i.e. output = offset + ( input × scale ). Various offset parameters are provided, which might be useful in different scenarios – they are all simply added together. Note that it is valid to use this algorithm with no input bus selected, in which case the output is simply the offset voltage.

parameters.name: Channels
parameters.description: The number of bus channels to process.

parameters.name: Enable
parameters.description: Enables the channel. Disabled channels have no effect on the bus.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

parameters.name: Semitones
parameters.description: Sets the channel offset (semitones i.e. 1/12th of a Volt).

output_ports.name: Output
output_ports.description: The output bus. If set to 'None', the input bus is used as output, and the mode is always 'Replace'.
